articles.name: Discord Self-Service Advertising Terms
articles.title: Discord Self-Service Advertising Terms
articles.body:
Effective Date:  July 1, 2026

These Discord Self-Service Advertising Terms (these “Self-Service Advertising Terms”) govern your access to and use of the Discord Self-Service Ads Services (the “Ads Services”) to create, manage, and run advertising on Discord. They are entered into by Discord Inc. (“Discord,” “we,” or “us”) and the entity that accepts them (“Advertiser,” “you,” or “your”).

You may access the Ads Services through the Ads Manager, Discord’s self-service advertising interface. References in these Self-Service Advertising Terms to the Ads Manager describe a means of accessing the Ads Services and do not limit these terms to interface-based access.

These Self-Service Advertising Terms are subject to, and incorporate by reference, the Advertising Business Services Terms (the “Business Terms”), together with the Ads Policy and, to the extent you use them, the Attribution & Measurement Terms, the Custom Audience Terms, and the Events API Terms. Capitalized terms not defined here have the meaning given in the Business Terms.

Any product-specific terms made available in or linked to these Self-Service Advertising Terms apply when you use that product and such product terms control over any general terms for that product to the extent of any conflict. These Self-Service Advertising Terms control over the Business Terms solely as to their subject matter. The Discord Advertising IAB Addendum governs managed advertising and does not apply to self-service advertising.

Section 1: Definitions

In these Self-Service Advertising Terms:

“Ad” means advertising or other commercial content you create, submit, or run through the Ads Services.

“Ads Account” means the advertising account established and maintained under a Team and used to configure, run, and pay for Ads Campaigns.

“Ads Manager” means Discord’s self-service advertising interface, as made available from time to time, through which you may access the Ads Services.

“Ads Services” means the self-service advertising services Discord makes available, including the Ads Manager, Ads-related APIs, and the other tools and interfaces through which you access them, whether through the interface, an API, or an authorized partner.

“Campaign” means an advertising campaign configured through the Ads Services.

“Order” means your submission through the Ads Services to run a Campaign, including its budget and, where applicable, bid.

“Reward” means the Discord Orbs or other in-platform reward Discord makes available to users in connection with advertising on Discord.

"Team" means your Discord team (the same team construct used across Discord's developer surfaces, including the Developer Portal) which you access using your Discord account and which serves as the account through which you accept these Self-Service Advertising Terms, provide and maintain your business information (such as your legal business name and tax identification), manage members and their roles, and establish and hold one or more Ads Accounts in the Ads Manager. Team roles, including the Owner and Admin roles, have the meaning given in the Developer Portal.

Section 2: Account Registration and Access

a. Eligibility and Registration. To use the Ads Manager, you must establish and maintain a Team and at least one Ads Account under that Team, and you must provide accurate and current business, registration, and billing information, including your legal business name and tax identification. You must be able to form a binding contract and meet any eligibility requirements Discord sets. Discord may approve, decline, or condition a Team or Ads Account in its discretion. A Team and its Ads Accounts may be established and operated by an Advertiser for itself, or by an agency or other authorized party on behalf of one or more Advertisers, as described below.

You accept these Self-Service Advertising Terms at the Team level, and your acceptance applies to the Team and every Ads Account under it. Only an individual who holds an Owner or Admin role on the Team — a role with authority to manage the Team and its settings — is authorized to accept these Self-Service Advertising Terms on the Team's behalf. By accepting, you represent that you hold an Owner or Admin role on the Team and that you have authority to bind the entity on whose behalf you accept; "you," "your," and "Advertiser" refer to that entity as well as to the individual accepting. Your acceptance binds that entity whether or not the Ads Manager restricts which role may accept.

If you accept these Self-Service Advertising Terms without authority to bind the entity on whose behalf you purport to accept, you are personally bound by, and liable under, these Self-Service Advertising Terms as the Advertiser for all Campaigns, Orders, charges, and other activity under the Team and its Ads Accounts, to the same extent as if you were the Advertiser.

b. Responsibility for the Team and Ads Account(s). You are responsible for your Team and all activity under it and under each of your Ads Accounts, including the acts and omissions of every individual, agency, or other party you authorize to access or operate your Team or any Ads Account, and all Campaigns, Orders, and charges. Keep your credentials secure and promptly tell Discord of any unauthorized use. You are also responsible for monitoring your Team and each Ads Account and all Campaigns, Orders, and modifications (whether made by you, by a party you authorize, or by Discord on your behalf) including all Campaign settings, targeting, budgets, bids, and creative, any settings or parameters configured on your behalf before you accept these Self-Service Advertising Terms, and any changes made during a Campaign, and for promptly reviewing your billing statements and charges for accuracy.

c. Authorized Users. You manage members and their access at the Team level through Team roles, and only an Owner or Admin may add, remove, or change the access of members. You may authorize individuals to access your Team and one or more Ads Accounts, and you are responsible for managing and revoking their access and for their compliance with the Terms. 

d. Advertisers Granting Access.  If you authorize an agency or other party to access or operate your Team or any Ads Account, for all or specific Campaigns, you remain responsible for all activity under your Team and Ads Accounts, you control the scope and revocation of that access, you authorize that party to submit Orders, instructions, and approvals within that scope, and you will ensure it complies with the Terms. Granting access does not reduce your obligations. As the Advertiser, you are responsible for establishing your Team and Ads Account(s) and for accepting these Self-Service Advertising Terms, and you remain responsible and liable for all acts and omissions of any agency or other party you authorize, and for all Campaigns, Orders, charges, and other activity under your Team and Ads Accounts, to the same extent as if you had taken those actions yourself. Where an agency or other party will act on your behalf, Discord may require that you establish the Team and Ads Account(s) and accept these Self-Service Advertising Terms, that the agency or other party separately accept these Self-Service Advertising Terms, and that you then authorize it to access and act within your Team and its Ads Accounts; if the Ads Manager does not support a particular authorization method, Discord may require alternative evidence of your authorization. At Discord's request, you will promptly provide written confirmation that you have authorized a party to act on your behalf and of the scope of that authorization.

e. Agencies Acting for Advertisers. If you establish or operate a Team or Ads Account, or run Campaigns, on behalf of one or more other parties (for example, as their agency), you represent for each that you are authorized to act for and bind that party to the Terms and that your acts and omissions are within the scope of your authority. Each such party is an "Advertiser" under the Self-Service Advertising Terms. You are responsible for each Advertiser's compliance with the Self-Service Advertising Terms. If you do not have authority to bind an Advertiser, or that Advertiser does not separately accept these Self-Service Advertising Terms, you are directly and personally bound by, and liable under, the Self-Service Advertising Terms as the Advertiser for all Campaigns, Orders, charges, and other activity associated with that Advertiser. At Discord's request, you will promptly provide written confirmation of your relationship with, and authority to act for and bind, each Advertiser, and Discord may require other reasonable evidence of your authority.

f. Multiple Advertisers. If you manage Campaigns for more than one Advertiser, you will accurately associate each Campaign and Order with the correct Advertiser, and the representations in this Section apply to each.

g. Reliance. Discord may treat any Order, instruction, or approval submitted through your Team or any Ads Account as authorized by you and by any Advertiser on whose behalf you act.

h. Discord-Operated Service. Where Ads Manager features are limited or unavailable, or at your request, Discord may access and operate your Team and Ads Account on your behalf, including to provide and configure your business, registration, and billing information, establish and manage your Ads Account, manage users and roles, and create, configure, submit Orders for, manage, optimize, or cancel Campaigns. Discord does so as a service and not as your agent; Discord's operation of your Team or Ads Account does not make Discord a party to, or liable as the Advertiser under, these Self-Service Advertising Terms, and does not reduce your obligations. You remain responsible and liable for your Team, your Ads Account, and all Campaigns, Orders, charges, settings, and other activity, whether configured or submitted by you or by Discord on your behalf (including before you accept these Self-Service Advertising Terms), to the same extent as if you had taken those actions yourself. Discord's operation does not substitute for acceptance of these Self-Service Advertising Terms by an Owner or Admin of your Team under Section 2(a). You may direct Discord to stop operating your Team or Ads Account at any time, and Discord may decline or discontinue this service in its discretion.

Section 3: Using the Ads Services

a. License. Subject to the Self-Service Advertising Terms, Discord grants you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to access and use the Ads Services to create and manage your own Campaigns. Discord reserves all rights not expressly granted.

b. Configuration. You configure Campaigns in the Ads Manager, including the product, available targeting, budget, and bid, and you are responsible for your configuration and for all Ads, content, and destinations.

c. Orders and Acceptance. When you submit an Order, you tell Discord the advertising you want to run, your spend, and, where applicable, your bid. Discord may accept or decline an Order and runs accepted Campaigns as inventory and availability allow.

d. Auction and Pricing. Charges are based on the applicable billing metric for the product, including, where applicable, your bid in an auction. Auction, bidding, and pricing mechanics are described in the Ads Manager or applicable documentation; product-specific pricing is in the product terms. Bids, rates, and Charges may vary and change over time based on the auction and other factors, and any modification to a Campaign may affect the applicable rate and your Charges.

e. Campaign Modifications. Unless otherwise agreed by the parties, you may modify a Campaign, including pausing or cancelling it, at any time through the Ads Manager. A modification or cancellation may take a period of time to take effect, and you will be liable for all Charges incurred until the modification or cancellation is effective.

f. Discord-Operated Campaigns. Some Ads Manager features may be limited or unavailable, and Discord may set up, manage, or optimize Campaigns on your behalf as a service; where it does, the Campaign remains subject to the Terms and your obligations are not reduced.  Discord may also access and operate your Ads Account on your behalf as provided in Section 2(h).

g. Ad Review and Approval. All Ads and Campaigns are subject to Discord’s review and the Ads Policy, and Discord may reject, remove, or decline to run any Ad or Campaign in its discretion.

h. Your Responsibility for Ads, Targeting, and Compliance. You are solely responsible for your Ads and ad materials, the products and services you advertise, your landing pages and other destinations, and your targeting, bidding, trafficking, and other Campaign decisions, including those you make or change in real time through the Ads Manager and without Discord’s review of each Campaign. You are responsible for ensuring that your Ads and Campaigns, and the foregoing, comply with the Self-Service Advertising Terms, the Ads Policy, and all applicable laws, and that all claims and information in your Ads are accurate and not misleading. Discord’s review of, or decision to run, an Ad or Campaign does not relieve you of these responsibilities or constitute approval of your compliance.

i. Automated and AI Features. Discord may make available automated or AI-assisted features that generate, suggest, or optimize creative, bids, budgets, targeting, or other Campaign settings. Any output of these features is generated by automated means and is not tested, verified, or guaranteed by Discord to be accurate, complete, or compliant. You are responsible for reviewing and approving any AI-generated or AI-suggested content, targeting, or modification before it is used in, or applied to, your Campaigns, and your use of these features does not reduce your responsibilities or obligations under the Terms, including those in the preceding subsection.

j. Content License. You grant Discord a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free, sublicensable license to host, use, display, reproduce, and adapt (including resizing) the Ads, content, and marks you provide, as needed to deliver your Campaigns and provide and improve the advertising services. You retain your content and marks; Discord retains the Ads Manager, Rewards, and its data and measurements.

k. Beta and Experimental Features. The Ads Manager, and some products, features, or campaign types, may be offered on a beta, pilot, or experimental basis, and Discord may test, change, or discontinue them. Beta and experimental features are provided “as is” and “as available,” may not function as described, and Discord has no liability for them beyond what the Self-Service Advertising Terms, or applicable product terms, provide.

l. Feedback. If you give Discord feedback, suggestions, or ideas about the Ads Manager or the advertising services, Discord may use them for any purpose without restriction or obligation to you, and you grant Discord a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide, royalty-free license to use and incorporate them.

m. Rewards. Where Discord makes a Reward available to users in connection with your advertising, the Reward is Discord Orbs unless Discord specifies otherwise. Discord Orbs are redeemable only within Discord, have no cash value, and are governed by the Discord Orbs Terms in Discord’s Paid Services Terms. Discord administers and delivers Rewards to users; you have no liability to users for Rewards, and your payment for advertising is not a payment to any user. Discord may modify, substitute, or discontinue Rewards, which will not affect amounts owed for advertising already delivered.

Section 4: Prohibited Uses

In addition to the restrictions in the Business Terms, you will not: use the Ads Services other than as permitted; interfere with or disrupt them; access them by unauthorized means or circumvent their controls; use any robot, spider, scraper, or other automated means, or any data extraction, data mining, or harvesting technique, to access the Ads Services or extract data from them except as Discord expressly permits; misrepresent your identity or authority; use them to build a competing product; or use data obtained through them except as the Terms permit, including the restriction on using such data to train any AI or machine-learning model.

Section 5: Fees and Payment

a. Charges. You will pay all charges for your Campaigns, based on the applicable billing metric and Discord’s measurements (Section 6).

b. Budgets and Spend Controls. You may set budgets and spend controls in the Ads Manager. Actual spend may vary from, and may exceed, a stated budget within a reasonable tolerance due to the timing of measurement and delivery, and Discord will not knowingly charge you more than your budget.

c. Invoicing. Unless otherwise agreed, Discord will invoice you, and you will pay undisputed amounts within thirty (30) days of the invoice date.

d. Credit and Invoiced Billing. Discord will bill your Campaigns on an invoiced basis as provided in subsection (c). Discord may obtain credit authorizations, set and adjust credit limits, and condition or require continued eligibility for invoiced billing in its discretion. Where you apply for or use invoiced billing, Discord may obtain business credit reports about you and use them to assess your eligibility and to set or adjust your credit limit. Discord may make additional payment methods available in the future, and if it does, the terms governing those methods will apply when they are made available to you.

e. Taxes. Amounts exclude any applicable taxes. You are responsible for any taxes, levies, or duties that apply to your Campaigns, including withholding taxes, (other than taxes on Discord’s net income,) and you will pay amounts due to Discord without reducing them for any such taxes. You will indemnify Discord for any claim arising from your failure to pay or remit taxes you owe.

f. Late Payment. If you do not pay undisputed amounts when due, Discord may charge interest on the overdue amount at 1.5% per month, or the maximum rate permitted by applicable law if lower, from the due date until paid. You will reimburse Discord’s reasonable costs of collection, including reasonable attorneys’ fees, and Discord may use a third party to collect overdue amounts.

g. Billing Disputes. If you believe an invoice or charge is incorrect, you must notify Discord within thirty (30) days after the invoice or charge date, with reasonable supporting detail; charges not disputed within that period are final. Except as required by law or as the Terms expressly provide (for example, a credit under a product term), amounts invoiced and paid are non-refundable.

h. Promotional Credits. Discord may offer promotional or advertising credits, which you may apply to eligible charges subject to any terms Discord specifies. Unless stated otherwise, credits are time-limited, have no cash value, are non-transferable, cannot be combined with other offers, and may be modified or revoked by Discord, and Discord’s decisions on the allocation and use of credits are final.

Section 6: Measurement and Reporting

Discord’s measurements are the authoritative basis for delivery and charges. Discord may provide reporting for your internal use, which is Discord’s Confidential Information and which you may disclose only as permitted in Section 10. While You may receive reports from approved measurement partners, such reports do not control billing, and Discord’s measurements control in the event of a discrepancy. Processing of personal data for measurement is governed by the Attribution and Measurement Terms. Where you, or an agency or other party acting on your behalf, transmit event, conversion, or similar data to Discord through the Discord Events API or a comparable integration in connection with Campaigns, the Events API Terms (or other terms Discord makes available for that integration) govern that data, together with these Self-Service Advertising Terms and the Attribution & Measurement Terms.

Section 7: No Guarantee; Placement Discretion

Self-service advertising is provided “as is” and “as available.” Discord does not guarantee any level of delivery, reach, results, or other outcomes, and estimates (including any estimated or projected CPM, CPC, or CPA rates, conversion volumes, audience sizes, and campaign performance) are not binding. Discord has sole discretion over whether, when, where, and to which users Ads are shown, including placement, targeting, frequency, and delivery, and may suspend or cease delivery at any time. Except as a product term expressly provides, Discord is not required to provide any makegood or credit for failing to deliver an estimated level of delivery.

Section 8: Invalid Activity

a. Discord may monitor for, identify, and exclude invalid, fraudulent, automated, or otherwise non-genuine activity affecting Campaigns (“Invalid Activity”), by automated or manual means. Invalid Activity includes activity generated by bots, automated scripts, emulators, or click farms; activity resulting from hijacked devices or falsified device, location, or identity signals; and activity that is fraudulent or generated through deception. Discord does not warrant that it will identify or exclude all Invalid Activity. 

b. Invalid Activity is not billable, and Discord’s determination is final for charges, subject to any reconciliation in the Terms or a product term. Product terms may add product-specific Invalid Activity provisions, which control for that product. Discord’s sole obligation with respect to Invalid Activity that affects charges is to adjust or credit amounts as set out in the Self-Service Advertising Terms or the applicable product terms, and Discord has no further liability for Invalid Activity, including activity it does not detect or filter.

c. You will reasonably cooperate with Discord in connection with the detection and investigation of Invalid Activity.

d. Where Discord makes a Reward available to users in connection with advertising, the fact that a Reward is offered, or that a user may be motivated by it, does not by itself make activity Invalid Activity; Invalid Activity instead means activity that is automated, fraudulent, falsified, or otherwise not a genuine user’s good-faith engagement. Discord may withhold or revoke Rewards, and exclude or adjust affected Charges, for Invalid Activity. You acknowledge that where Discord makes a Reward available to users in connection with advertising, any related inventory is incentivized and that Discord’s measurements and reporting may identify and account for it as such.

Section 9: Suspension and Termination

Discord may suspend, limit, or terminate your access to the Ads Services, any Campaign, or these terms as set forth in the Business Terms or for any violation of the Terms. You may stop using the Ads Services at any time. You remain responsible for amounts incurred before termination, and provisions that should survive will survive.

Section 10: Confidentiality

Without limiting the Business Terms, you will not disclose Discord’s non-public pricing, bid or auction information, or non-public performance or reporting data relating to your use of the Ads Services, except as the Business Terms permit. Notwithstanding the foregoing, you may disclose performance and reporting data relating to your own Campaigns to your service providers and measurement partners acting on your behalf, provided that Discord has approved any measurement partner that will receive such data (such approval not to be unreasonably withheld), solely to measure, verify, or analyze the performance of your Campaigns and subject to confidentiality obligations at least as protective as these Self-Service Advertising Terms, and you remain responsible for each recipient's compliance. You will not publish that data publicly or disclose it to any other advertiser, agency, or advertising network, and you will require each recipient to use it solely to provide measurement, verification, or analysis to you and not to further disclose it. You, your service providers, and your measurement partners will retain that data only as long as needed for those purposes and will delete it when it is no longer needed. This Section does not permit disclosure of Discord's Attribution Results, which are governed by the Attribution & Measurement Terms.

Section 11: Representations and Warranties

In addition to the Business Terms, you represent and warrant that: (a) you have the right and authority to enter into the Self-Service Advertising Terms and to advertise the products, services, and destinations in your Campaigns; (b) you own or have all rights, licenses, and consents necessary to provide the Ads, content, and marks you submit and to grant the license in Section 3; (c) your Ads, content, and destinations comply with the Self-Service Advertising Terms and applicable law and do not infringe or misappropriate any third-party rights; (d) the information you provide is accurate; and (e) you will not engage in, encourage, or facilitate Invalid Activity, and your destinations and applications are not designed or operated to generate it; and (f) any measurement or Events API integration you use is implemented and operated in accordance with such products respective terms, Discord’s technical specifications and documentation, and the event data you transmit is accurate, complete, and not generated by automated, fraudulent, or deceptive means.

Section 12: Other Terms

a. Indemnification; Liability. The indemnification and limitation of liability provisions of the Business Terms apply to these Self-Service Advertising Terms and your use of the Ads Services, as well as any self-service advertising products. Without limiting those provisions, you will indemnify Discord for claims arising out of your Ads, content, marks, and destinations, your targeting, bidding, and trafficking decisions, your landing pages and destination URLs, the data you transmit to Discord (including through the Events API), the products and services you advertise, and your use of the Ads Services, except to the extent such claim is caused by Discord.

b. Disputes; Governing Law. The dispute resolution, governing law, and related provisions of the Business Terms apply to these Self-Service Advertising Terms and your use of the Ads Services, including the arbitration agreement and class-action waiver and the governing-law and venue provisions set out there. To the extent the Business Terms allow you to opt out of arbitration within a stated period measured from a signed insertion order or its effective date, for self-service advertising that period instead runs from the date you first accept these Self-Service Advertising Terms.

c. Modifications. Discord may modify these Self-Service Advertising Terms as set forth in the Business Terms, and your continued use of the Ads Manager or Ad Services after the effective date is considered acceptance.

d. Entire Agreement. These Self-Service Advertising Terms, including any Supplemental Ads Terms under Section 13, supplement the Business Terms and the other Terms and are the entire agreement on self-service advertising, superseding any prior agreement on that subject.

e. Notices and Electronic Communications. Discord may give you notices about the Ads Manager and these terms through the Ads Manager, by email to your account address, or by other reasonable means, and you consent to receive communications, including invoices, electronically. You will keep your account contact and billing information accurate and current.

Section 13: Supplemental Ads Products and Services 

You may choose to use products, services, features, and tools that Discord makes available to supplement the Ads Services (collectively, “Supplemental Ads Products and Services”). Discord may modify, suspend, terminate access to, or discontinue any Supplemental Ads Product and Service at any time in its discretion. If you use any Supplemental Ads Product and Service, your use is subject to the additional terms and policies Discord makes available for it, and your continued use indicates your acceptance of those terms and policies (collectively, “Supplemental Ads Terms”). A Supplemental Ads Term controls over these Self-Service Advertising Terms for the applicable Supplemental Ads Product and Service to the extent of any conflict.

Discord’s Supplemental Ads Products and Services and the applicable Supplemental Ads Terms include:

Discord-Operated Services (Discord setting up, managing, optimizing, or operating your Team, Ads Account, or Campaigns on your behalf): governed by Section 2(h) and Section 3 of these Self-Service Advertising Terms.

Data tools that import data to, or export data from, the Ads Services (including Custom Audiences, measurement and reporting tools, pixels, postback URLs, and software development kits): governed by the Attribution & Measurement Terms, or the Custom Audience Terms, as applicable.

Ads-related APIs (including the Events API and any other advertising API Discord makes available): governed by the Events API Terms (or other terms Discord makes available for that API) and the applicable API documentation.

You agree to be bound by these Self-Service Advertising Terms and any applicable Supplemental Ads Terms when you access or use the applicable Supplemental Ads Product and Service.

articles.name: Events API Terms
articles.title: Events API Terms
articles.body:
Effective Date:  July 1, 2026

This Events API License (the “EAPI Terms”) governs the Partner’s (“you”, “your”, “Partner”) use of the Events API. The EAPI Terms supplement the Discord Developer Terms of Service (the “Developer Terms”), the Advertising Business Services Terms (the “Business Terms”), the Attribution and Measurement Terms (the “Attribution Terms”); and any applicable terms or conditions in an Insertion Order(s) (each, an “IO”) between the parties (collectively with the Developer Terms, the Business Terms, the Attribution Terms, and any other applicable Discord terms and policies, the “Terms”). Capitalized terms used but not defined in these EAPI Terms have the meanings given in the applicable Terms. In the event of a conflict between these EAPI Terms and any of the Terms, the EAPI Terms will control solely with respect to the subject matter of such conflict. The EAPI Terms apply to Partner Data from the point at which Partner first transmits such data to Discord via EAPI. For the avoidance of doubt, the Developer Terms govern Partner’s access to and use of EAPI as an API, and these EAPI Terms, Business Terms and Attribution Terms govern the advertising and data-related aspects of Partner’s use of EAPI. To the extent Partner transmits data to Discord via EAPI that would otherwise be governed by the Business Terms, any other terms, or any IO, these Terms shall control with respect to such data.

Section 1: Definitions

For purposes of these EAPI Terms, the following definitions apply in addition to those in the Terms:

“Attribution Results” has the meaning set forth in the Attribution Terms.

“EAPI” or “Events API” means Discord’s server-to-server application programming interface that enables Partner to transmit Partner Data directly to Discord for the purposes described in these EAPI Terms.

“Event Data” has the meaning set forth in the Attribution Terms.

“Matching Attributes” means Personal Data (which can include, but is not limited to, hashed emails, hashed phone numbers, hashed mobile identifiers) provided by Partner to Discord via EAPI that enables Discord to match individuals with Discord user accounts for the purpose of targeting advertising or other communications to those matched users on the Discord platform.

“Partner Data” means any data transmitted by Partner to Discord via EAPI, including, but not limited to, Event Data and Matching Attributes.

Section 2: Purpose and EAPI License

a. Features

Discord is making EAPI available to you to transmit Partner Data to Discord via a server-to-server integration. EAPI may include, but is not limited to, the following capabilities:

  1. Transmission of Event Data for attribution, conversion tracking, analytics, and campaign optimization;
  2. Transmission of Matching Attributes for audience matching and targeting; and
  3. Transmission of other data types.

You acknowledge that the EAPI features may be changed or removed at Discord’s sole discretion. Partner also acknowledges that EAPI is provided “AS IS” without warranties of any kind. Discord makes no representations regarding EAPI's availability, accuracy, or fitness for any particular purpose. Partner assumes all risk associated with its use of EAPI.

b. Purpose

Discord may use Partner Data, alone or in combination with Discord's own data and other information, for: (i) providing advertising services; (ii) developing and providing campaign attribution, conversion tracking, analytics, insights, and metrics (including generating Attribution Results as described in the Attribution Terms); (iii) creating and managing custom audiences for targeting; (iv) targeting and optimizing campaigns; (v) optimizing Discord's systems and services; and (vi) developing and improving Discord's products and services (collectively, the “Purpose”).

c. License

Subject to your compliance with the Terms, Discord grants you a limited, non-exclusive, non-sublicensable, non-transferable, non-assignable, revocable license to access and use EAPI solely as necessary to transmit Partner Data to Discord, to the extent permitted under the Terms. Discord reserves all rights, title, and interest not expressly granted herein.

d. Activation and Cross-Campaign Application

The EAPI Terms apply when you first transmit Partner Data to Discord via EAPI. Once activated, these EAPI Terms govern all Partner Data transmitted to Discord by or through EAPI, and to the extent of any conflict, controls over the Business Terms and any applicable IO with respect to such data. For clarity: (1) EAPI serves as a persistent delivery mechanism for all Partner Data transmitted to Discord by or through EAPI; (2) Partner Data transmitted via EAPI shall be treated according to the Purpose of these EAPI Terms, including for all Ad campaigns whether or not identified in applicable IO(s) between the parties, or whether such IO(s) are in effect as of the Effective Date or entered thereafter; and, (3) if Partner transmits data to Discord using both EAPI and other delivery methods (e.g., through an Attribution Partner), these EAPI Terms govern only the data transmitted via EAPI. Data delivered through other methods continues to be governed by the Attribution Terms, Custom Audience Terms, or other terms, as applicable.

Section 3: Data Provisions

To the extent Partner transmits Partner Data to Discord via EAPI, each party shall act as an independent Controller with respect to such Partner Data under these EAPI Terms. Discord may use and combine Partner Data with Discord’s own data and other information for use consistent with the Purpose. Without limiting the foregoing, all Partner Data transmitted via EAPI is subject to the data protection obligations set forth in Section 4 of the Attribution Terms (Roles, Data Protection Obligations).

Section 4: Partner Obligations

a. Integration and Compliance

Partner will integrate with EAPI in accordance with applicable documentation made available by Discord and will comply with all applicable rate limits, technical specifications, and security requirements provided to Partner in writing. You are solely responsible for the accuracy, completeness, and lawfulness of all Partner Data transmitted via EAPI.

b. Representations and Warranties

With respect to all Partner Data transmitted via EAPI (including Event Data and Matching Attributes), you represent and warrant that you: (i) have obtained all necessary consents, permissions, and authorizations from Data Subjects, or otherwise have a valid legal basis under Applicable Data Protection Laws, to collect and transmit the Partner Data to Discord for the purposes described in these EAPI Terms and the Terms; (ii) have provided appropriate privacy notices to your users informing them that their data may be shared with Discord for Discord’s business purposes; (iii) will maintain appropriate records of consent or other legal bases as required by Applicable Data Protection Laws; (iv) have implemented appropriate technical and organizational measures to protect the Partner Data; (v) will notify Discord without undue delay upon becoming aware of any Personal Data breach affecting Partner Data; (vi) will ensure that Partner Data does not contain any sensitive information or special category data as defined under Applicable Data Protection Laws, including health information, financial information, or information about racial or ethnic origin, religious beliefs, or sex life or sexual preferences; (vii) will not transmit any Personal Data relating to users under the age of 13 or the applicable age of consent under Applicable Data Protection Laws in the jurisdiction where such data was collected, whichever is greater; (viii) will promptly notify Discord of any complaints relating to Partner Data and will reasonably cooperate with Discord in responding to such complaints; and (ix) will only transmit Partner Data that is necessary for and consistent with the Purpose.

c. Indemnification

You shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Discord and its affiliates, directors, officers, employees, and agents against all claims, liabilities, costs, expenses, damages and losses (including any direct, indirect or consequential losses, loss of profit, loss of reputation and all interest, penalties and legal costs and all other reasonable professional costs and expenses) (“Claims”) arising from or related to: (i) your breach of Applicable Data Protection Laws relating to Partner Data; (ii) your collection, processing, or transmission of Partner Data to Discord (including any claim made by a Data Subject or supervisory authority with respect thereto); (iii) any breach of the representations and warranties set forth above; or (iv) any claim that your provision of Partner Data to Discord violates any third-party rights or Applicable Data Protection Laws.

Section 5: Data Retention & Deletion

Discord shall retain Matching Attributes only as necessary to provide the advertising services, and will delete the Matching Attributes promptly after the matching process, upon termination of these EAPI Terms, or upon your written request. Discord may retain Matching Attributes only as required by law, with notice to you where permitted, maintaining confidentiality and limiting use to legal compliance purposes.

articles.name: Discord Nitro 10K Orbs Offer
articles.title: Discord Nitro 10K Orbs Offer
articles.body:
Until July 14, 2026, eligible Discord users who subscribe to Nitro will receive 10,000 Orbs! Read below to learn more.

This Nitro Orbs Offer is only available to a limited number of eligible users. If you don't see any promotion details on your Nitro tab in the desktop app or browser, it is because you do not meet the eligibility criteria. 

If you are not eligible for the promotion, the Orbs will not be applied to your account.

What this article covers:

  • Promotion Summary
  • What is Discord Nitro?
  • What are Discord Orbs?
  • How do I subscribe to Nitro?
  • Frequently Asked Questions
Note: Please make sure your Discord app is updated to the latest version! If you don’t see the promotion offer in your app after you update, you are not eligible for this promotion

Promotion Summary

Offer Details: Until July 14, 2026, eligible users who sign up for Discord Nitro (Monthly or Yearly) through the Discord desktop app will receive 10,000 Orbs! You can learn more about Discord Orbs here.

Eligibility: This promotion is available to a limited number of users. Eligible users will see a notification and/or promotional banner in the Nitro tab in the Desktop app.

Deadline for Claiming Promo: This promotion must be claimed before July 14, 2026 at 4pm PT.

What is Discord Nitro?

Discord Nitro is our subscription plan that unlocks a world of perks that’ll make your time on Discord as fun as ever! Personalize and enhance your experience with popular perks like HD streaming, 500MB file uploads, profile and avatar customizations, and more:

  • Custom & Animated Emojis
  • Custom Profiles
  • HD Streaming
  • Discord App Color Themes
  • Unlimited Soundboard Access & Entry Sounds
  • Extended Quest Rewards
  • Special Shop Perks
  • More Nitro & Nitro Basic perks

What are Discord Orbs?

Discord Orbs are virtual rewards typically obtained by completing Quests. With Orbs, you can purchase items in the Shop and Orb-exclusive items to personalize your Discord profile. Orbs added to your wallet on Discord do not expire! Check out the following support articles to learn more about:

  • Shop FAQ
  • Discord Quests FAQ
  • Discord Orbs FAQ
When you use Discord Orbs, you agree to the Discord Orbs Terms in our Paid Services Terms.

How do I subscribe to Nitro?

If you’re eligible for this Nitro offer, you can head over to the Nitro Tab.

Step 1: Go to the Nitro tab

On the desktop or browser app, press the Direct Messages button in the top left corner and then select the Nitro tab. Alternatively, you can navigate to your User Settings > Nitro.

Step 2: Subscribe to Nitro Monthly or Yearly

Once you are on the Nitro page, you’ll see the offer promotion banner. Press Claim Now.

Nitro Offer on Desktop

Step 3: Start Your Nitro Subscription

You’ll be asked to select either a monthly or yearly plan for Nitro.

Then, on the next screen, enter your credit card information and other payment details. 

Step 4: Receive Your 10,000 Orbs

Upon successful completion of sign up, the orbs will automatically be added to your orb wallet in the Shop tab and you can now purchase Shop and Orb-exclusive items!

A notification to let you know that the 10k orbs offer was added to your wallet in Shop after subscribing to Nitro.

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Why don't I see this offer in my Discord app?

A: If you don't see any promotion details on your Nitro tab across the desktop, browser, or mobile apps, it is because you do not meet the eligibility criteria.

Q: Why do I see a temporary hold/charge on my account?

A: There will be a temporary hold placed on the payment method upon activating the promotion. This is to help Discord confirm that the submitted payment method is valid. This charge will appear in your Discord transaction history, and it may appear as a hold on your payment method.

After you subscribe, we reverse the temporary hold to return the funds, and you’ll see that reflected in your Discord transaction history. Your bank may take longer to remove the hold from your payment method.

Note: Banks will usually remove the temporary hold from your account within a few business days.

If your Discord transaction history does not show the hold released after 5 business days, please let us know at https://dis.gd/contact, and our support team will be able to investigate this issue further.

articles.name: Discord on Meta Quest FAQ
articles.title: Discord on Meta Quest FAQ
articles.body:
Discord is available to download directly from the Meta Horizon Store — bringing the full Discord experience to your Meta Quest headset.

What this article covers:

  • Is Discord Available on Meta Quest?
  • How to Download Discord on Meta Quest
  • What Discord Features Are Available on Meta Quest?
  • Frequently Asked Questions

Is Discord Available on Meta Quest?

If you’re a Meta Quest user, you can use Discord while in your VR experience. Through Meta Quest, you can access the full Discord app, like your servers, direct messages (DMs),  group DMs, voice/video calls, without needing a separate device or using the VR headset web browser.

You can get Discord on your Meta Quest by downloading the app through the Meta Horizon Store.

A Discord server as seen in the Discord app on a Meta Quest device.

How to Download Discord on Meta Quest

Discord is available to download from the Meta Horizon Store. You can find it directly from your headset or through the Meta website.

1. Open the Meta Horizon Store on your Meta Quest headset, or visit the Meta Horizon Store page on your web browser.

2. Select Install (or Get) to install Discord on your headset.

3. Once installed, open the Discord app and log in to your existing Discord account, or create a new account if you don't have one yet.

The Discord app on the Meta Horizon Store for installation.

Note: Note: If you’re having issues with the Meta Horizon store or downloading the Discord app to your Meta Quest, please reach out to Meta Support.

What Discord Features Are Available on Meta Quest?

Discord on Meta Quest includes all the features you're used to from the Discord app, including:

  • Your servers, DMs, and Group DMs
  • Voice calls using your headset's built-in microphone
  • Video calls if you’ve set up a Meta Avatar
  • Streaming Meta Quest gameplay to your friend on Discord

Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Do I need a new Discord account to use Discord on Meta Quest?

A: No. You can log in with your existing Discord account. If you don't have one yet, you can create a new account directly from the app on your headset.

Q: Is Discord on Meta Quest free to download?

A: Yes. Discord is free to download from the Meta Horizon Store. Some features require a Discord Nitro subscription.

Q: Where can I download Discord for my Meta Quest headset?

A: You can find the Discord app in the Meta Horizon Store on your headset or on your browser. 

Q: What Meta Quest devices is Discord available on?

A: Discord is available to use on the following Meta Quest devices: Quest 2, Quest Pro, and Quest 3 series.

Q: Can I use Discord voice and video calls while in VR?

A: Yes. Because Meta Quest headsets have a built-in microphone, you can join Discord voice and video calls directly from your headset without needing a separate device. You can also use your Meta Avatar in video calls if you’ve created one.

Q: I’m having issues with my Discord app on my Meta Quest device.

A: If you’re having issues with the Discord app itself, please reach out to our Support team. If you’re having issues with your Meta Quest device, please reach out to Meta Support.

Q: Does Meta see my activity in Discord because I use the app on Meta Quest?

A: No. Using the Discord app on Meta Quest is like using Discord on any other supported device.

Q: How does Discord use my data when I use the Discord app on Meta Quest?

A: Discord uses your data consistent with our Privacy Policy, like on any other device.

articles.name: Discord Gift Cards
articles.title: Discord Gift Cards
articles.body:
Discord gift cards are an easy way to treat yourself or a friend. Redeem one to add balance to your account and spend it on Nitro, Shop items, Server Boosts, and more. 

What this article covers:

  • What are Discord gift cards?
  • Where can I buy a Discord gift card?
  • What can I buy with a Discord gift card?
  • How to Redeem Your Gift Card
    • Desktop App
    • Discord.com/redeem
    • Mobile Availability
  • Frequently Asked Questions
    • Buying Gift Cards
    • Redeeming
    • Your Balance & Spending
    • Account & Policies
    • Other
Important: Discord gift cards are currently available at select online retailers in the United States. We'll be expanding availability over time, so check back for updates.

What are Discord gift cards?

Discord gift cards are a prepaid card you can use to make purchases on Discord. Once redeemed, the balance is added directly to your account and can be spent across items in Discord.

Gift cards are a great option if you:

  • Don't have a credit card or prefer not to link one to your account
  • Want a flexible way to pay for Nitro, Shop items, or other Discord purchases
  • Want to set a budget and add a specific amount to your account before spending
  • Are a parent or friend who wants to give the gift of Discord to someone in your life 

Where can I buy a Discord gift card?

Discord gift cards are available at select online retailers in the United States. You can find more information on where to buy here.

What can I buy with a Discord gift card?

Once redeemed, you can spend your gift card balance on things like: 

  • Nitro and Nitro Basic - Discord's premium subscription plans.
  • Nitro Gifts - Buying Nitro for your friends.
  • The Discord Shop - Avatar Decorations, Profile Effects, Nameplates, and more. 
  • Server Boosts - Support your favorite servers and help unlock perks for the whole community.
  • Game Shops - Purchase in-game items for select titles directly through Discord.

Discord Gift Card Balance

How to Redeem Your Gift Card

You can redeem your gift card in two ways: through the discord.com/redeem web page, or directly in the desktop app via Billing.

Important: Make sure you're logged into the correct account before redeeming. Gift card balances cannot be transferred between accounts once redeemed.

Desktop App

1. Open the Discord desktop app and select the cogwheel [] next to your username to open User Settings.

2. Head to User Settings > Billing > Payment Methods, and select the Redeem button by the Gift Card Balance option.

3. Enter your gift card code exactly as shown and follow the on-screen prompts. 

4. Press Redeem. Your balance will be added to your account immediately.

Redeeming a Discord Gift Card through Billing on Desktop

Discord.com/redeem

1. Head to discord.com/redeem. You can do this on any mobile, desktop, or tablet device.

2. Log in to your Discord account, or create one if you're new.

3. Enter your gift card code exactly as provided and follow the on-screen prompts.

4. Press Redeem. Your updated balance will appear on screen.

5. Select Get Nitro or Get Nitro Basic to start spending your balance, or head to the desktop app to explore more options in the Shop.

Discord.com/redeem Landing Page

Mobile Availability

Redeeming a gift card and spending your balance aren’t available in the mobile app at this time.

You can still redeem a gift card and purchase items using a browser on your mobile phone:

  • Redeem your gift card at discord.com/redeem. 
  • After redeeming, open Discord in your browser at discord.com to spend on things like Nitro, the Shop, and more. 
  • Your purchases will be available across devices on your account.
  • You can view your balance in the web app as well.

  

Redeeming a Discord Gift Card through Mobile Web Browser

Frequently Asked Questions

This FAQ is divided into sections:

  • Buying Gift Cards
  • Redeeming
  • Your Balance & Spending
  • Account & Policies
  • Other

Buying Gift Cards

Q: Are digital and physical gift cards available? 

A: Digital gift cards are available in the US at select retailers. Physical cards are coming soon, check out our Help Center for the latest on where to buy.

Q: What countries are Discord gift cards available in?

A: Digital gift cards are only available in the US, and we have more cooking…stay tuned!

Q: Where can I buy Discord gift cards?

A: Digital gift cards are available in the US at select retailers. Physical cards are coming soon, check out our Help Center for the latest on where to buy.

Q: What gift card amounts are available?

A: Discord gift cards are available in $25 and $50 options, or a choose your adventure option where you can pick between $15–$150. Availability may vary by retailer.

Q: Can I buy a gift card for someone in another country?

A: Gift cards can only be redeemed in the country they were purchased in. To redeem, your account's Country/Region setting must match the gift card's currency.

Redeeming

Q: Do I need an account to redeem my gift card?

A: Yep! You must be logged into a Discord account to redeem a gift card. Don’t have one yet? It’s free to sign up at Discord.com.

Q: Can I redeem a gift card from another country?

A: Gift cards can only be redeemed in the country they were purchased in. To redeem, your account's Country/Region setting must match the gift card's currency.

Q: Why isn't my gift card code working?

A: Let’s help with that! Here are some pro tips and considerations:

  • Typo? Make sure you're entering the code exactly as shown on the back of the card.
  • Wrong Country? Gift cards can only be redeemed in the country they were purchased in. If your Country/Region doesn't match the card's currency, redemption will be blocked. You can see what region your account is set to under Country/Region in User Settings > Billing > Payment Methods.
  • Balance or redemption limit reached. Your account may have reached its wallet balance or redemption limit.
  • Platform pro tip. Just a heads up, you can only view and spend your balance on the desktop app or web browser.

If none of the above apply and your code still isn't working, please contact Discord support with your card details and a photo of the back of the card.

Q: I’m still having issues with my Discord gift card. What do I do? 

A: If you're still experiencing issues with your gift card, you can submit a request through the Discord support form. 

Visit dis.gd/billing and select Refunds, Cancellations & Payments. From there, choose Gift Cards under the Type of Billing Issue and complete the rest of the form based on your specific concern.

Your Balance & Spending

Q: Where can I see my gift card balance?

A:  You can check your balance anytime under User Settings > Billing > Payment Methods within the desktop app or web browser.

Q: Does my gift card or balance expire?
A: Discord gift cards and balances in the United States do not expire. Expiration terms vary by country in other regions. See our Gift Card Terms and Wallet Terms for more information.

Q: Can I redeem multiple gift cards?

A: Yes! You can redeem multiple gift cards to your Discord account, each one will add to your existing gift card balance up to the maximum balance limit in your country.

Q: Is there a limit for how much money I can have in my account?

A: Yes, there is a limit! If you have multi-factor authentication on your account, you can have a higher balance. 

Don’t have MFA set up? You should! Check out how to set up MFA here.

Q: Can I use my gift card balance on mobile?

A: You can view and use your gift card balance on desktop app and web browser. Anything you buy will show up across all your devices.

Q: Are there any cases where I cannot use my gift card balance?

A: Yes. Your gift card balance may be temporarily unavailable if your wallet has been frozen, or if you have reached your gift card balance limit. If you believe this is an error, please contact Discord support.

Q: Is there anything I can’t buy with a Discord gift card?

A: You can spend your Discord gift card balance on most purchasable things in Discord - like Nitro, the Shop, Server Boosts, Gifts on Discord, and more. The option to use your balance won’t show up during check-out if you can’t use a gift card balance to purchase that item.

Q: Where can I see my gift card balance?

You can check your balance anytime in the desktop or web app under User Settings > Billing.

Q: Can I use my gift card balance and another payment method together in one purchase?

A: Your gift card balance must cover the cost of your purchase to use. Otherwise, you’ll need to use another payment method on file for purchase at this time. We don't currently support splitting a payment between your gift card balance and another payment method, but we're working on it! 

Q: What happens if my subscription renews and my gift card balance is too low?

A: If your Gift Card balance is insufficient at renewal, you’ll have 3 days to update your payment method before it expires. Discord won't automatically charge another payment method, so make sure your balance is topped up before your renewal date. You can update your payment method anytime in User Settings > Billing Settings > Subscriptions.

Account & Policies 

Q: Can I transfer my gift card balance to another account?

A: No. Gift card balances are tied to the account they were redeemed on and cannot be transferred.

Q: What happens to my gift card balance if I delete my account?

A: If you delete your Discord account, any remaining gift card balance may be forfeited and cannot be recovered. More information can be found in the Wallet Terms. 

Q: What happens if my Discord account is suspended or terminated?

A: Use of Discord is subject to our Terms of Service, Community Guidelines, and other policies. Violating these terms may result in action on your account, including suspension or termination. If your account is suspended, you will not have access to your gift card balance for the duration of the suspension. See our Gift Card Terms and Wallet Terms for more information.

Q: What should I do if I think my gift card was stolen or used fraudulently?

A: Discord can assist with suspicious activity on your account, such as an unknown charge using your gift card balance. You’ll need to work with the retailer for any issues with your gift card prior to redemption.

If you suspect a fraudulent purchase was made using your Discord gift card balance, you can reach out to our support team here. Please fill out the ticket with the following categories:

  • What can we help you with?: Refunds, Cancellations & Payments
  • Type of Billing Issue?: I have a question/issue regarding an unauthorized transaction

Q: Can I get a refund for a purchase made with a Discord gift card?

A: If a purchase made with your gift card balance qualifies for a refund under Discord's standard refund policy, the refund will be returned to your account balance, not to another payment method.

Other

Q: Who is CIMI / Weary Travelers Inc.?

CIMI is the company we work with to bring gift cards to you. Weary Travelers, Inc. is the issuer of our gift cards. Someone at Discord (shhh, it was Byron) thought that Weary Travelers was a fun name - so rest here weary traveler. You have seen too many FAQs. Sit by the fire before you continue. When you’re ready, you can find more information in the Gift Card Terms and Wallet Terms.

Discord gift cards are available at select retailers online. The list below is a great place to start to find a retailer near you or shop online.
